subject: Accounting
title: WorldCom's $11 Billion Lie
description: A 60-second lesson on WorldCom's $11 Billion Lie.

hook.text: WorldCom turned operating expenses into capital assets with a few journal entries. $11 billion worth.

buildup.text: Line costs—the fees WorldCom paid to use other networks—were its biggest operating expense.

discovery.text: By reclassifying expenses as assets, WorldCom inflated profits and hid its real financial state.

twist.text: The fraud was discovered by an internal auditor, Cynthia Cooper, working nights to avoid detection.

climax.text: WorldCom filed the largest bankruptcy in U.S. history at the time—$107 billion in assets.

punchline.text: One reclassification. Eleven billion in fraud.
